2604.19678.title: Exploring Language-Agnosticity in Function Vectors: A Case Study in Machine Translation
2604.19678.paper_summary: Function vectors (FVs) are vector representations of tasks extracted from model activations during in-context learning. While prior work has shown that multilingual model representations can be language-agnostic, it remains unclear whether the same holds for function vectors. We study whether FVs exhibit language-agnosticity, using machine translation as a case study. Across three decoder-only multilingual LLMs, we find that translation FVs extracted from a single English$\rightarrow$Target direction transfer to other target languages, consistently improving the rank of correct translation tokens across multiple unseen languages. Ablation results show that removing the FV degrades translation across languages with limited impact on unrelated tasks. We further show that base-model FVs transfer to instruction-tuned variants and partially generalize from word-level to sentence-level translation.
2604.19678.paper_summary_zh: 功能向量（FVs）是從模型激活中提取的任務的向量表示，這些激活發生在上下文學習期間。雖然先前的研究顯示多語言模型表示可以是語言無關的，但目前尚不清楚這是否也適用於功能向量。我們研究功能向量是否顯示出語言無關性，並以機器翻譯作為案例研究。在三個僅解碼的多語言LLM中，我們發現從單一的英語$\rightarrow$目標方向提取的翻譯功能向量能夠轉移到其他目標語言，並持續改善在多個未見語言中正確翻譯標記的排名。消融結果顯示，移除功能向量會降低語言間的翻譯效果，對無關任務的影響有限。我們進一步顯示基模型功能向量可以轉移到指令調整的變體，並在從單詞級到句子級翻譯中部分泛化。
2604.19678.author: Nurkhan Laiyk et.al.
2604.19678.authors: Nurkhan Laiyk, Gerard I. Gállego, Javier Ferrando, Fajri Koto
